Backscattering factor due to alu-foils was found to be
constant for beta-rays of nuclides measured.
All Kr and Xe atoms were retained in the foil.
No diffusion of implanted atoms was detected.
Contamination: found at distance of 5 mass numbers and
more. Therefore only measured up to Kr-94 and Xe-143.

Decay data used in the present work
Rb-90: although 2 isomers were known with half lives
of 2.6 and 4.2 min, the authors observed only one
component of 3.15 min, assumed that it corresponded
to the 2.6 min isomer and suggested a lower limit
of 15 for the isomeric ratio to the 4.2 min isomer.
